column2: General term for systems that monitor and control industrial processes (e.g., SCADA, DCS).
High-level system for large-scale, geographically distributed industrial monitoring and control.
Ruggedized computer used for real-time control of specific machinery in OT.
Electronic device that connects sensors/actuators to a SCADA master station.
Hardware/software that detects or causes change through direct monitoring/control of physical devices.
Commonly used ICS serial protocol; lacks native authentication and encryption.
Protocol used in utilities (electric/water) for communication between master and outstations.
Architecture extending cloud computing to the edge; processing at a local gateway/node.
Data processing performed at the immediate source of data (e.g., on the IoT device itself).
The graphical interface used by operators to interact with and monitor an industrial process.
